comment: Quality Gates catalog -- docs-repro dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/docs-repro/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/docs-repro/.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container with cargo + python3 + curl pre-installed and the workspace cargo build complete, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh exits 0
the run leaves a partial-clone working tree at /tmp/reposix-example-01
the example pushes a commit and the simulator's audit log shows a helper_push_* row

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh regressed OR the sim/cli/helper changed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver -- real blocker was container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim; it now starts an ephemeral sim on the host and runs the example container with --network host. QL-001 is landed (P91-02); the 'cold container build budget' cause was moot (the gate runs on host with warm binaries). Check examples/01-shell-loop/expected-output.md + target/debug binaries.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the example did not work end to end, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh would exit non-zero OR the simulator's audit log would lack a helper_push_* row; either directly falsifies the 'exits 0 and pushes' claim. The gate now runs the example against a driver-started sim reachable via --network host, so a real failure surfaces as a non-zero container exit.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container, python3 examples/02-python-agent/run.py exits 0
the agent finds 'database' in some issue body and applies a severity label
git push reports success

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/02-python-agent/run.py regressed OR the sim/cli/helper changed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host) and (b) run.py globbing '*.md' at repo root instead of the canonical 'issues/*.md' (QL-001), which matched 0 records yet exited 0 -- a vacuous-GREEN honesty hazard now closed by a fail-loud zero-match guard. QL-001 is landed (P91-02). Keep the python stdlib-only constraint.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the agent did not apply a severity label and push, run.py would exit non-zero (it now fails loud on a zero 'database' match instead of exiting 0 on a no-op) OR 'git push' would report failure; either falsifies the 'finds database + labels + pushes' asserts. The container gate greps the captured stdout for a non-zero match count and a push summary.

asserts: examples/03-claude-code-skill/RUN.md exists and references .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow
examples/03-claude-code-skill/agent-prompt.md exists and parses as markdown
this row is graded by manual verifier-subagent dispatch (not auto-runnable)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/03-claude-code-skill/{RUN.md, agent-prompt.md} got out of sync with .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow

asserts: bash examples/04-conflict-resolve/run.sh exits 0 in ubuntu:24.04 container
the second agent observes 'fetch first' from helper stderr and recovers via git pull --rebase
after recovery, both edits are merged

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/04-conflict-resolve/run.sh regressed OR push-time conflict detection regressed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host), (b) an 'ls *.md' glob at repo root instead of canonical 'issues/*.md' (QL-001), (c) agents A and B SHARING one reposix cache -- which masked the conflict (A's push advanced the shared base so B's stale push looked current); fixed by giving each agent its own REPOSIX_CACHE_DIR, and (d) the rebase recovery not deleting the fast-import staging ref refs/reposix-import/main before re-fetch. Push-time conflict detection is NOT regressed (proven once the cache split let it fire). QL-001 is landed (P91-02).
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If conflict recovery did not work, the second (stale-base) agent's push would be accepted instead of rejected (no 'fetch first'), OR the rebase-and-retry would fail to land both edits; either falsifies the conflict-then-recover asserts. With per-agent caches the helper now emits 'local base version: 1, backend version: 2 ... Run: git pull --rebase' and rejects B, then the recovery re-pushes clean.

rows.comment: P124/SC1 (DRAIN-22): expected.asserts REWRITTEN 2026-07-18 to the real-runtime-error contract -- example-05 now drives the actual BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT helper stderr refusal under REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT and recovers via `git sparse-checkout set`. This SUPERSEDES the 2026-07-13 honesty reword (quick 260713-q0e) that had narrowed the asserts to the pre-emptive sparse-checkout + source-constant stand-in on the then-true premise that run.sh never read helper stderr; P124 Wave 1b makes the run drive the real error, so that caveat no longer holds. Status flipped to NOT-VERIFIED pending the Wave 1b run.sh rework + a real post-release container re-grade (do not leave a stale PASS describing the old pre-emptive-only behavior). minted_at preserved (write-once).

asserts: bash examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh exits 0 in an ubuntu:24.04 container after driving a REAL runtime blob-limit-exceeded refusal under REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT and recovering
the helper emits the BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT stderr refusal (naming `git sparse-checkout set`) when a blob-materializing fetch/checkout of refs/reposix/origin/main would exceed REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT
after `git sparse-checkout set` narrows the scope, the retried checkout materializes the narrowed issues/*.md record set and completes 0 -- the observe-error-then-recover cycle

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh regressed OR the blob-limit guard constant / sparse-checkout path regressed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host) and (b) 'git sparse-checkout set 0001.md ...' using the OLD zero-padded flat pathspecs -- canonical is 'issues/1.md' (QL-001, unpadded + bucketed); the 'ls' inspection lines had the same drift. QL-001 is landed (P91-02). HONESTY REWORD (2026-07-13, quick 260713-q0e): asserts #2/#3 were reworded to the TRUTH -- example-05 does NOT observe a runtime blob-limit stderr error (the fast-import fetch path bypasses the per-RPC check; expected-output.md documents zero blob_limit_exceeded rows). It exercises the PRE-EMPTIVE git sparse-checkout pattern + the source-constant presence ONLY. The real runtime-error recovery is covered by quality/gates/agent-ux/dark-factory.sh; a deeper example-05 fix (exercise the real runtime error + recovery cycle) is filed for v0.15.0 (SURPRISES-INTAKE). SUPERSEDED 2026-07-18 (P124/SC1, DRAIN-22): run.sh IS being reworked (Wave 1b) to DRIVE the real runtime BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT stderr refusal + git sparse-checkout recovery, so the 'observes the error from stderr and recovers' claim is now CORRECT and the rewritten expected.asserts assert exactly that; the earlier 'never reads helper stderr' instruction applied only to the pre-P124 pre-emptive-only run.sh. Status is NOT-VERIFIED until a real post-release container run re-grades the reworked example.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If example-05 did NOT drive the real runtime blob-limit error and recover, the row's three rewritten asserts would each land in asserts_failed: assert 1 requires `bash examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh` to exit 0 in an ubuntu:24.04 container ONLY after a real REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT-triggered refusal is observed and recovered, so a run that never triggered the error (or failed to recover) exits non-zero under set -euo pipefail; assert 2 requires the captured helper stderr to contain the BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT refusal naming `git sparse-checkout set` (run.sh greps its captured stderr for that token + [RPX-0503] and fails loud if absent), so a fast-import path that silently bypassed the per-RPC command=fetch check would falsify it; assert 3 requires the retried checkout AFTER `git sparse-checkout set` to materialize the narrowed issues/*.md set and exit 0, so a recovery that did not narrow-then-complete fails the bare 'ls issues/*.md' under set -e. The row is NOT-VERIFIED until a real post-release container run re-grades it -- no PASS is asserted on the old pre-emptive-only behavior.

sources: (historical: scripts/repro-quickstart.sh -- migrated to this gate by SIMPLIFY-06; see commit history)

asserts: step 2: simulator starts and listens on 127.0.0.1:7878
step 3: reposix init sim::demo /tmp/repo exits 0 and configures partial-clone
step 4: git checkout -B main refs/reposix/origin/main exits 0
step 5: issues/1.md exists with title: frontmatter
step 7: git push reports main -> main
step 8: audit_events_cache has a row with op LIKE 'helper_push_%'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/tutorials/first-run.md or tutorial-replay.sh drifted. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver -- BOTH cited causes were wrong: (1) the 'cold container build budget' was moot because the gate runs ON HOST with warm binaries (verifier.container corrected to null, kind:mechanical), and (2) QL-001 was already landed (P91-02) and push works on git 2.25.1. The REAL bugs were harness-local: the sim bound :7780 while 'reposix init' targets the default :7878 (now bound :7878); step 5 asserted a stale root '0001.md' (canonical is issues/1.md); and step 7 grepped stdout for 'main -> main' though git writes it to STDERR (now greps combined output). Runs hermetically under its own REPOSIX_CACHE_DIR.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the tutorial did not replay clean, one of steps 2-8 (sim up, init exit 0, checkout, issues/1.md materialized with title, push 'main -> main', helper_push_% audit row) would fail and be recorded in asserts_failed; each maps 1:1 to a green-contract assert. The harness now exits 0 with all 7 asserts_passed on a real run.

asserts: docs/benchmarks/latency.md frontmatter last_measured_at is < 30 days old
the cached-read p50 cell value matches the headline 8 ms to within +/-2ms

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/latency.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the headline 8ms cached-read claim were false, either assert would falsify it: a stale last_measured_at (>=30d) means the reviewer can no longer trust the recorded number and must FAIL pending re-measurement, and a p50 cell value outside 8ms+/-2ms directly contradicts the docs/index.md and README.md headline text the row exists to police.

asserts: headline 89.1% is greppable from docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md
the comparison fixture file referenced from token-economy.md exists

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the 89.1% token-reduction headline were false, the reviewer's grep for the exact figure in docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md would come back empty (the number was never actually printed where the headline claims it lives), or the comparison fixture backing the calculation would be missing -- either finding directly falsifies the claim and flips the row to FAIL rather than PASS.

asserts: every fenced code block in user-facing docs has a catalog row in quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible.json or is matched by an allow-list entry
no catalogued snippet drifted from its source (sha256 match)

the allow-list-mode escalation advisory fires on the UNCOVERED block count exceeding PIVOT_THRESHOLD (50), not the raw block count -- an allow-list or catalog entry can retire the advisory (GTH-V15-49 Option B, 2026-07-16)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a doc edit added a fenced code block without a catalog row OR a catalogued row's source content drifted; fix by either pasting the --write-template output or rolling back the doc edit. If the new block is illustrative, add the derived_id to quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible-allowlist.json with a reason. If RED is the '>50 uncovered blocks' escalation advisory: it counts UNCOVERED blocks only (GTH-V15-49 Option B, 2026-07-16) -- cataloguing or allow-listing the individual uncovered blocks (required anyway to clear the itemized failures) also clears this advisory; do NOT bump PIVOT_THRESHOLD.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the pivot regressed to counting RAW blocks again (the pre-GTH-V15-49 bug), a doc corpus with block_count=51 and uncovered_count=0 would incorrectly fire the '>50 uncovered blocks' escalation advisory even though every block is covered or allow-listed; snippet-extract.py --check's pivot logic asserts on len(uncovered), not len(blocks) (test_pivot_counts_uncovered_not_raw_blocks pins this), so a raw-count regression would flip a currently-passing corpus to a false RED, directly falsifying the 'pivots on uncovered, not raw block count' claim in expected.asserts.

rows.comment: P124/SC1 (DRAIN-22, closes F-K4b) -- container-rehearse.sh must EARN each `kind: container` row's congruence by HARVESTING per-step `ASSERT-PASS:` lines from the container's own stdout, NEVER by copying row.expected.asserts verbatim into asserts_passed on a bare `exit 0`. Today a no-op `exit 0` script passes congruence identically to a real one (container-rehearse.sh:96-107/:187-193), so the per-expected-assert congruence gate (_audit_field.py::asserts_congruent) is a TAUTOLOGY. This meta-check proves the tautology is closed: the verbatim-copy path is grep-absent AND a no-op fixture FAILS congruence while a real one passes. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P124 Wave 0 catalog-first commit; verifier ships in Wave 1a.

asserts: container-rehearse.sh contains no code path that copies row.expected.asserts into asserts_passed (grep-proven absence of the verbatim-copy line)

a fixture example that exits 0 but prints ZERO `ASSERT-PASS:` lines yields an EMPTY harvested asserts_passed, which the explicit non-empty guard (`if not passed: return False`) rejects as NOT congruent -- asserts_congruent() itself no-ops True on an empty list, so that guard (not asserts_congruent) is what closes the zero-line tautology
a fixture example that prints one `ASSERT-PASS:` line per expected.assert produces a congruent artifact

rows.owner_hint: if RED: container-rehearse.sh still copies row.expected.asserts into asserts_passed (delete the verbatim-copy path and HARVEST `^ASSERT-PASS: ` lines from container stdout instead -- see quality/gates/docs-repro/tutorial-replay.sh for the per-step-earned pattern), OR a container example stopped emitting one ASSERT-PASS line per expected.assert. The named verifier ships in P124 Wave 1a; NOT-VERIFIED until then (catalog-first).
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If container-rehearse.sh regressed to copying row.expected.asserts verbatim into asserts_passed (the F-K4b tautology this row closes), the meta-check's three asserts would falsify the earned-congruence claim: assert 1 greps container-rehearse.sh and FAILS if any verbatim-copy line survives (a grep-proven presence of the copy path); assert 2 runs a fixture example that exits 0 while printing ZERO ASSERT-PASS lines through the harvest logic and FAILS unless the explicit non-empty guard (`if not passed: return False`) rejects it as NOT congruent -- asserts_congruent() itself no-ops True on an empty asserts_passed, so that guard (not asserts_congruent) is what forbids a no-op exit-0 script from satisfying earned congruence; assert 3 runs a fixture that prints one ASSERT-PASS line per expected.assert and FAILS unless the artifact is congruent. A tautological harness would make the no-op fixture pass congruence, tripping assert 2, so a false green cannot ride on it. The static grep-absence leg runs docker-free; the dynamic legs degrade to NOT-VERIFIED (never FAIL) where docker is absent, per OP-2.

rows.comment: P124/SC2 (DRAIN-23) -- container-rehearse.sh backgrounds the ephemeral sim and tears it down via a bash `EXIT` trap that NEVER fires when the runner's subprocess.run(timeout=) SIGKILLs the harness (reproduced in the b773c04 CI failure: an orphaned pid left on host port 7878), and its readiness check (:135-141) curls whatever answers on 7878 so a stale orphan is silently reused. This row's selftest proves teardown is SIGKILL-proof (process-group kill + an internal `timeout` strictly shorter than the row's catalog timeout_s) and that a pre-run port-7878-free check is fail-loud. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P124 Wave 0 catalog-first commit; verifier ships in Wave 2.

asserts: container-rehearse.sh wraps its `docker run` in an internal `timeout` strictly shorter than the row's catalog timeout_s so it reaps children before an outer SIGKILL
the ephemeral sim runs in its own process group and teardown kills the GROUP (setsid/set -m), not just the leader pid
a pre-`docker run` check FAILS LOUD (NOT-VERIFIED/error, not silent reuse) when port 7878 is already occupied by a stale sim
a selftest that SIGKILLs the harness mid-run leaves no listener on 127.0.0.1:7878 afterward

rows.owner_hint: if RED: container-rehearse.sh teardown leaked a sim on 127.0.0.1:7878 or silently reused a stale orphan -- start the sim in its own process group and `kill -TERM -- -$PGID` the group, wrap `docker run` in `timeout <timeout_s - margin>`, and add a pre-run port-7878-free fail-loud gate (`kill $(lsof -ti:7878)` recovery named in the error). The named verifier ships in P124 Wave 2; NOT-VERIFIED until then (catalog-first).
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If container-rehearse.sh's teardown were not SIGKILL-proof (the b773c04 orphan-on-7878 bug), the four asserts would falsify the claim: assert 1 greps the harness and FAILS unless `docker run` is wrapped in an internal `timeout` strictly shorter than the row's catalog timeout_s (so the harness reaps its own children before the runner's outer subprocess.run(timeout=) SIGKILLs it); assert 2 FAILS unless the ephemeral sim is started in its own process group and teardown kills the GROUP (setsid/set -m), not just the leader pid; assert 3 FAILS unless a pre-`docker run` check refuses a stale sim already bound on 7878 fail-loud instead of silently reusing it; assert 4 runs a selftest that SIGKILLs the harness mid-run and FAILS unless no listener survives on 127.0.0.1:7878 afterward. A leaked orphan would leave a listener and trip assert 4. The selftest degrades to NOT-VERIFIED (never FAIL) where docker/lsof substrate is absent, per OP-2.

rows.comment: P124/SC4 (DRAIN-13) -- container-rehearse.sh currently `exit "$EXIT_CODE"` where $EXIT_CODE == the docker rc (:164/:199), so after Wave 1 a docker rc=0 can co-exist with an artifact exit_code=1 (a missing required ASSERT-PASS line -> congruence fail recorded in the artifact) and the harness would falsely exit 0. This row's selftest proves the harness writes the artifact FIRST, re-reads exit_code from it, and exits with THAT value, and that the pre-run readiness gate requires sim reachability (not merely a bound port -- the DRAIN-13 sim-readiness-race leg). Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P124 Wave 0 catalog-first commit; verifier ships in Wave 4.

asserts: container-rehearse.sh writes the artifact FIRST, then re-reads exit_code from it and exits with THAT value
a forced artifact exit_code=1 while docker rc would be 0 makes the harness exit 1 (selftest-proven)
the pre-`docker run` readiness gate also requires sim reachability, not merely a bound port

rows.owner_hint: if RED: container-rehearse.sh exits on the docker rc instead of the persisted artifact exit_code (a rc=0 can mask an artifact exit_code=1) -- write the artifact first, re-read its exit_code, and `exit` with that; also make the pre-run readiness gate require sim reachability, not just a bound port. The named verifier ships in P124 Wave 4; NOT-VERIFIED until then (catalog-first).
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the harness derived its exit from the docker rc instead of the persisted artifact exit_code (the DRAIN-13 gap where rc=0 masks an artifact exit_code=1), the three asserts would falsify the claim: assert 1 FAILS unless container-rehearse.sh writes the artifact FIRST and then re-reads exit_code from it and exits with THAT value; assert 2 forces an artifact exit_code=1 while docker rc would be 0 and FAILS unless the harness process exits 1 (selftest-proven); assert 3 FAILS unless the pre-`docker run` readiness gate requires real sim reachability (a successful curl against the sim the harness started), not merely a bound port -- the DRAIN-13 sim-readiness-race leg. A harness that exited on docker rc would exit 0 on the forced-fail fixture, tripping assert 2. Degrades to NOT-VERIFIED (never FAIL) where docker substrate is absent, per OP-2.
